response.justification: Candidate is a strong full-stack software engineer with web, mobile, and some embedded experience, but the role requires deep hardware/SoC expertise: SoC and IP modeling, AI accelerator performance analysis, hardware-software co-design, SIMD/MIMD, and GPU architecture. The resume shows no evidence of chip-level modeling, deep learning workload optimization, or accelerator architecture work. The brief C/C++ stint at National Instruments and embedded mentions do not approach the hardware architecture depth required for a Principal Performance Architect.
strengths: Long, varied engineering career across multiple stacks and domains
Some low-level C/C++ and embedded exposure (National Instruments, robotic arms)
Strong software craftsmanship discipline (TDD, CI/CD, performance-sensitive systems at Sociomantic)
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling or hardware architecture experience
No AI accelerator, deep learning workload, or GPU architecture background
No evidence of SIMD/MIMD work or hardware-software co-design at the principal level

key_factors.factor: No AI accelerator or DNN optimization background
